short_name: Precision Biomarkers to Detect Brain Injury in Active-Duty United States Special Operations Forces With Repeated Blast Exposure
title: Precision Biomarkers to Detect Brain Injury in Active-Duty United States Special Operations Forces With Repeated Blast Exposure

skipped_criteria: Active-duty Navy SEAL Service Member: no schema field exists for military branch, unit, or occupational specialty.
Early career SEAL (0-2 years) or mid-late career SEAL (10-15 years): no schema field exists for career stage or years of service.

History of moderate or severe TBI: the schema's presumed_tbi bool cannot distinguish severity or historical diagnosis; partially encoded as a soft exclusion but severity and historical nature cannot be captured.
History of major neurological disorder: no schema field exists for prior neurological history.
Untreated or unstable severe psychiatric condition: no schema field exists for psychiatric history or treatment status.
Current severe medical condition that requires long-term treatment: no schema field exists for chronic medical conditions or treatment requirements.
Any condition that may cause undue risk or logistical contraindication: too vague and general to encode in the schema.
MRI contraindication: no schema field exists for imaging contraindications.
PET contraindication: no schema field exists for imaging contraindications.
